requiredLabs.action: Dose adjustment required. INR > 4.0: hold warfarin + consider vitamin K. INR < 1.5: increase dose.
Digoxin toxicity: nausea, visual changes, arrhythmia. Hold digoxin if level > 2.0 ng/mL. In elderly, target 0.5-0.9 ng/mL only.
requiredLabs.source: Beers Criteria 2023; ACC/AHA Heart Failure Guidelines 2022

requiredLabs.action: Hypokalemia potentiates digoxin toxicity. Correct potassium before adjusting digoxin dose.
requiredLabs.source: FDA Digoxin label; AHA HF Guidelines 2022

requiredLabs.action: Lithium toxicity: tremor, confusion, ataxia, renal failure. Level > 1.5 mEq/L: reduce dose. Level > 2.0 mEq/L: emergency hemodialysis.
requiredLabs.source: FDA Lithium label; APA Practice Guideline for Bipolar Disorder 2023

requiredLabs.action: Lithium causes hypothyroidism in 20-42% of patients. Biannual TSH monitoring required.
Reduce lithium dose significantly when eGFR < 60. Avoid if eGFR < 30. Lithium is nephrotoxic with chronic use.
requiredLabs.source: FDA Lithium label; KDIGO CKD Guidelines

requiredLabs.action: Methotrexate hepatotoxicity: hold if ALT > 3x ULN (typically > 120 IU/L). Consider liver biopsy after cumulative dose > 1.5g.
Methotrexate causes bone marrow suppression. Monitor for leukopenia (WBC < 3.0), thrombocytopenia (PLT < 100k).
Amiodarone causes thyroid dysfunction (hypo or hyperthyroidism) in 15-20% of patients. Biannual TSH required.
Amiodarone hepatotoxicity: hold if ALT > 3x ULN. Can cause severe hepatic failure.
Monitor renal function as iodine load from amiodarone can affect kidneys.
Phenytoin toxicity: nystagmus, ataxia, confusion. Narrow therapeutic index. In elderly/hypoalbuminemia, always check FREE phenytoin.
